core_loop.one_sentence: Aim and fire weapons from your mobile fortress to destroy the enemy's fortress before they destroy yours.
core_loop.player_goal: Reduce the enemy castle's health to 0%.
core_loop.failure_or_pressure: The enemy castle continuously fires back, reducing your health.
core_loop.why_it_is_fun: Satisfying physics-based destruction and the tactical choice of aiming at specific weak points.

player_controls.result: Aims a trajectory line and fires a projectile upon release

visible_ui.purpose: Show current health status of both castles
Assist the player in aiming their shot

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Establishes the threat and failure condition
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Creates immediate urgency for the player to act

timeline_beats.event: Player aims and fires a rocket
timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Demonstrates the core aiming and shooting mechanic
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: The primary interaction the user will perform

timeline_beats.gameplay_meaning: Shows the result of the player's action (damage and destruction)
timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Provides the satisfying payoff and visual feedback
Could be used as a power-up or secondary interaction in a playable
The final screen directing the user to the app store

mechanics.name: Drag-to-Aim Slingshot
mechanics.description: Player drags backwards from a character to set angle and power, visualized by a dotted line.

mechanics.name: Destructible Environment
mechanics.description: Castles break apart dynamically where projectiles hit.

mechanics.name: Multiple Attack Types
mechanics.description: Different characters fire different projectiles (single rocket, multi-rocket, laser).

feedback_and_juice.visual_or_audio_feedback: Explosions, flying debris, screen shake, floating damage numbers.
Dotted trajectory line with a crosshair at the end.
Smoke trails from projectiles, slight recoil on the firing character/castle.

must_include: One player character to shoot from
Basic destruction visual effects on impact
can_simplify: Enemy AI (make them stationary or fire at fixed intervals)
Complex physics (use pre-defined breakable chunks instead of dynamic slicing)
Multiple characters (stick to one weapon type for simplicity)
should_avoid: Waiting for enemy turns (keep the action continuous or player-driven)

variation_hooks.effect: Changes the visual and area of effect of the player's attack.
variation_hooks.safe_range_or_values: Single explosive, scatter shot, piercing laser
Tall tower, wide bunker, multi-tiered castle

variation_hooks.effect: Increases the perceived difficulty and urgency.
variation_hooks.safe_range_or_values: Player starts at 30% health vs Enemy at 100%

open_questions_or_assumptions: Assuming the castles are stationary and only move slightly due to physics impacts, not player-controlled movement.
Assuming the core loop is level-based where you destroy one castle and move to the next.
Is the damage purely based on hit location, or do different materials have different health?
